description_long: This object is a double cigarette, featuring two cylindrical sticks joined together along their length. Each cigarette has a white paper-wrapped body with a brownish filter at one end, and the filters are slightly rounded and textured, resembling cork. The paper appears smooth and clean, with no visible branding or markings. The tips opposite the filters are unburned, showing no signs of ash or charring. The cigarettes are tightly aligned side by side, giving the impression of a twin-stick form. The overall appearance is clean and unused, with a clear distinction between the white paper and the brown filter ends.
description: A pair of clean, unused cigarettes joined side by side, each with a white paper body and a brown filter tip.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the double cigarette, with both white paper bodies and brown filters visible.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the two round brown filters closely aligned together.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the white paper bodies and brown filters from the other side.
description_view_3: View from the filter end, showing the two brown filters side by side and the cylindrical shape of the cigarettes.
